results.title: Exxon Mobil vs. Chevron: Tracking Revenue Scale and Stability Over Two Years | The Motley Fool
Better Buy for 2026: ExxonMobil or Chevron? | The Motley Fool
Exxon vs. Chevron - Which Oil Giant Is a Buy for 2026?

results.summary: Both Chevron (CVX) and ExxonMobil report Q1 2026 earnings on May 1, 2026. ExxonMobil has materially higher revenue scale ($82.9B avg quarterly vs. Chevron $47.3B). Analysts see Chevron slightly better positioned for lower oil price environment due to lower capital intensity and stronger cash flow durability. XOM targets $25B in earnings growth and $35B in cash flow growth vs. 2024 levels. Sector-wide headwinds from ~39% YoY EPS decline due to lower realized commodity prices. BP and Shell historically more dividend-vulnerable (both cut in 2020); XOM and CVX maintained dividends.
